1.description: Inflation history in above- and below-median homeownership countries. Inflation data are from the Household Finance and Consumption Survey (HFCS). Above- and below-median homeownership based on average country homeownership rate across all three HFCS waves. The figure plots the mean and range of inflation across listed countries. Inflation in the figure is capped from above at 30% and from below at 0%. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: What do you think are good reasons for buying a home?The figure summarizes survey responses from 700 homeowners in Germany, Ireland, Italy, Portugal, and Spain. Respondents were asked to select all options that apply. The order of options was randomized. The figure shows the percent of respondents selecting each option and 95% confidence intervals. SeeInternet AppendixSectionIIfor more details. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Distribution of experienced inflation. This figure plots the distribution of experienced inflation (left panel) and log experienced inflation (right panel) in the HFCS sample. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: Aggregate homeownership rates by experienced inflation. This figure provides a scatter plot of country average log experienced inflation (x-axis) and homeownership rate (y-axis). Size indicates average population during the survey years and is obtained from the World Bank. The line shows the population-weighted logit fit of a regression of homeownership on country-average log experienced inflation. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

5.description: Hypothetical homeownership rates with alternate inflation histories. This figure summarizes actual homeownership from the HFCS data. Hypothetical homeownership rates are calculated using the model estimated in TableIII, column (3), assuming another country's inflation history. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

6.description: Binned scatter plots of residual homeownership and inflation experiences (HFCS). This figure provides binned scatter plots of measures of experienced inflation (x-axis) and residual homeownership (y-axis) in the HFCS data. In both plots, residual homeownership is calculated as the difference between actual homeownership (one or zero) and the predicted likelihood of ownership estimated from a logit regression of ownership on all demographic controls and survey-wave fixed effects. Thex-axis is experienced inflation in Panel A and log experienced inflation in Panel B. Households are divided into bins by ranking of their experienced inflation level. For each bin, we plot the average of thex- andy-axis variables. The line shows the linear fit. All calculations are weighted by the HFCS representative weights. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
